description:
From /critical-review of specled_-csg, consensus finding F3 [Medium; idioms+simplicity+performance]. Entry.normalized_findings/1 calls SpecLedEx.write_state to a temp file in System.tmp_dir!, re-reads it with Json.read, deletes it — purely to normalize findings. Extract the normalization as a pure function and call it directly; also removes the System.unique_integer tmp-path collision surface across concurrent worktree checks (security M re-scope landed here too).
Files: lib/specled_ex/evidence/entry.ex:88-104.
Full report: /Users/mz/vaults/rmbrs-vault/code_review/critical/specled_ex/2026-07-22/specled_-csg-evidence-ledger-split/20260722-062318-consolidated.md

comments.text:
ADMISSION SCOPE (orchestrator, run-2 W1)
Allowed touches:
- lib/specled_ex/evidence/entry.ex
- test/specled_ex/evidence/** (entry tests)
- .spec/specs/evidence_store.spec.md (verification stubs only, if needed)
Out of scope (intent): extract normalization as a pure function called directly — no temp-file round-trip, no System.unique_integer tmp paths. If the pure function must live outside entry.ex (e.g. shared with SpecLedEx.write_state), disclose the extra file in IMPLEMENTED rather than shipping silently. Do not touch sync.ex/tree_hash.ex/store.ex (owned by specled_-737 this wave).

IMPLEMENTED
Session: specled_-csg-evidence-ledger-split - implement (delegated impl-only worker, specled_-pqs)
Log: n/a (interactive delegated session, no dedicated log path supplied by orchestrator)

Commit: 79baa55
Files touched: lib/specled_ex/evidence/entry.ex
Tests added: none — existing test/specled_ex/evidence/entry_test.exs coverage
  ("builds canonical JSON with verification outcomes and normalized findings")
  already exercises the severity->level, subject_id->entity_id, and maybe_put
  nil-filtering behavior end to end and continues to pass unchanged against
  the pure implementation.
Verification stubs flipped: none (no execute:false stub existed for this
  behavior; spec statements specled.evidence_store.per_entry_isolation and
  .run_stamp_wins already have passing coverage, unaffected by this refactor)
Verification tier: change-scoped — test/specled_ex/evidence/entry_test.exs (3/3
  pass), mix test --stale (992 tests, 0 failures), mix spec.check
  --no-run-commands (status=pass errors=0 warnings=0; the 1 reported finding is
  an :info branch_guard_missing_subject_update note pre-existing on the epic
  branch for lib/mix/tasks/spec.check.ex, unrelated to this change); full-system
  proof deferred to the merge gate / PR CI
Final-tier runs: 1
Audit-expanded scope: none
Deferred must requirements: none

Summary: Replaced the temp-file round-trip in Entry.normalized_findings/1
(write via SpecLedEx.write_state to System.tmp_dir!, re-read via Json.read,
delete) with an equivalent pure in-memory transform inlined in entry.ex
(normalized_findings/1 + private maybe_put/3 and stable_sort/2 helpers). The
new code reproduces the exact same finding shape and stable sort order
(file, entity_id, code, message, index) that SpecLedEx.normalize_findings/1
already produced via the round trip, without touching disk, eliminating the
System.unique_integer tmp-path collision surface across concurrent worktree
checks. Kept the extraction local to entry.ex rather than sharing a module
with SpecLedEx.write_state, since the admission scope restricts touches to
entry.ex/test/spec files and the two call sites don't need to share code to
stay behaviorally identical — no extra files were touched.

VERIFY: PASS
Session: 07261362-0355-433e-83a4-d8c98524294e
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/07261362-0355-433e-83a4-d8c98524294e.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 1 (lib/specled_ex/evidence/entry.ex)
- Files outside Allowed globs: 0
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0 (sync.ex/tree_hash.ex/store.ex untouched)

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 0, passed: 0 (implementer flipped none; the evidence_store tagged_tests stubs were already execute:true and unaffected by this refactor)
- Requirements coverage (ticket's own contract — Entry.normalized_findings/1 becomes a pure in-memory transform, byte-identical to the old temp-file round-trip, no temp-file writes remain):
  - pure in-memory transform: covered by static read of entry.ex:88-124 + grep for write_state/tmp_dir/unique_integer/System.tmp in entry.ex (0 matches). Would fail if any disk round-trip remained. Seams: none.
  - byte-identical output (field mapping): covered by test/specled_ex/evidence/entry_test.exs:55-63. Would fail if severity->level, subject_id->entity_id, or file preservation diverged (asserts the exact finding map). The inlined transform is a line-for-line copy of SpecLedEx.normalize_findings/1 (lib/specled_ex.ex:189-215); the only textual change is sort-key access value_for(finding,"file") -> finding["file"], which is provably equivalent for the string-keyed finding maps constructed here (value_for checks the string key first, and no atom keys exist on these maps). stable_sort/maybe_put are byte-identical copies. The old path JSON-round-tripped this same list, which is lossless for string keys / string values / nil, so the direct in-memory result equals the old output as data and therefore as canonical-encoded bytes. Seams: none.
  - no temp-file writes remain: covered by grep (0 matches for write_state/tmp_dir!/System.unique_integer/unique_state_path). Would fail if a temp write path survived. Seams: none.
- Intent-level scope concerns: none. Diff removes the SpecLedEx.write_state -> Json.read -> File.rm round-trip and the System.unique_integer tmp-path helper (unique_state_path), matching the admission intent exactly. Kept inline in entry.ex; no extra file shipped, consistent with the IMPLEMENTED disclosure.
- Targeted verification: PASS. mix test test/specled_ex/evidence/entry_test.exs -> 3 tests, 0 failures. mix compile --warnings-as-errors -> clean. Flipped stubs: none.
- Full-suite verification: not run by verifier (by design)
- Audit-expanded scope (step 11): none

Verdict: PASS

Notes (advisory only — do not change the verdict):
- The covering test uses a single, fully-populated finding, so it does not exercise the multi-finding stable_sort ordering path or the maybe_put nil-drop branch (finding with nil file/subject_id). These paths ship as byte-identical copies of SpecLedEx.normalize_findings/1's helpers, and no edit within this diff can make them diverge from the old round-trip output, so the coverage gap is not reachable by this ticket's code paths. If future work wants a fail-capable guard on ordering/nil-drop, an entry_test case with 2+ findings and a nil-field finding would close it — out of scope here.

AUDIT: APPROVE (auto — selective audit)
Session: 07261362-0355-433e-83a4-d8c98524294e
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/07261362-0355-433e-83a4-d8c98524294e.jsonl

Reasoning: VERIFY reported PASS with full stub and requirement coverage, zero
scope violations, passing targeted verification, complete seam disclosure, and
no seam substituting production wiring. No auditor was spawned per policy.
APPROVED. Merged into specled_-csg/evidence-ledger-split. Closing per orchestrator sign-off.

title: review-F3: Evidence.Entry round-trips findings through a temp state file for a pure transform
